_comment: CANONICAL NAVIG tier / entitlement source of truth for the whole monorepo. See docs/BUSINESS-PLAN.md (the NAVIG Harbor plan). The `legacy` section below is the shipped 7-tier model; Python (navig-core/navig/license/quota.py) and TypeScript (navig-api/src/lib/billing.ts, navig-deck/lib/license.ts) all mirror it and MUST agree. The parity test navig-core/tests/license/test_tiers_parity.py enforces this. The `harbor` section is the TARGET model and is NOT yet wired into checkout — do not consume it at runtime until /monetization sets prices and the migration ships.

legacy._comment: Shipped 7-tier model. Mirrors quota.py + billing.ts + license.ts. `plus` is the consumer rung (1 host, buys `echo`); it exists in quota.py/license.ts but historically was MISSING from billing.ts (the drift this spec + parity test close). `echo` is granted by tier, never sold a-la-carte.

harbor._comment: TARGET model — the NAVIG Harbor consolidation. Personal Free/Plus/Max + B2B Team/Enterprise. Prices are hypotheses (run /monetization). The a-la-carte module SKUs collapse: capabilities are folded into Harbor Tiers but the module names survive as INTERNAL capability flags (the @requires_capability gate + APP_MODULE map are unchanged). org/seats/role are reserved so the B2B Team track is additive (never a reprice). Nothing here is consumed at runtime yet.

legacy_to_harbor._comment: DRAFT migration map — every existing tier maps to a Harbor Tier of >= value (additive, no one loses entitlements). Finalize in /monetization before the migration grant runs.
